skill.name: context-receipts
skill.description: Emit privacy-safe receipts for context selection, deferral, hydration, compaction, pruning, delegation, usage attribution, and boundary handoffs.
skill.summary: Privacy-safe context receipt patterns for AI agent workflows. Emit structured audit events that prove boundary behavior without exposing private content.
skill.author: caioribeiroclw-pixel

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill containing markdown and JSONL receipt pattern examples. All 82 static findings are false positives: backtick patterns are markdown code formatting, path sequences are relative documentation links, and hash references use SHA-256. No executable code, network access, or credential handling present.

content.user_title: Generate Privacy-Safe Context Receipts for AI Agents
content.value_statement: AI agent workflows handle context selection, hydration, and compaction in ways that are hard to audit. This skill provides structured receipt patterns that prove boundary behavior without exposing private content like prompts, schemas, or customer data.
actual_capabilities: Define JSONL event schemas for tool index loading, search, hydration, and call completion
Specify privacy defaults that exclude raw prompts, schemas, args, and results from receipts
Provide smoke test patterns for runtime MCP tool-surface diff detection
Emit context attention receipts that prove delivery, acknowledgment, and citation of required context
Define usage attribution events for token budget breakdowns by category
Specify pruning and compaction transaction safety receipts including rollback evidence
limitations: Documentation-only skill with no executable code beyond example shell commands
Requires the user agent or harness to actually emit the structured events
Does not provide semantic correctness verification, only boundary behavior proof
Receipt quality depends on consistent event emission by the underlying agent framework

use_cases.title: MCP Tool Search Verification
use_cases.description: Verify that lazy tool loading actually suppresses unused schemas and only hydrates selected tool definitions, with token bucket evidence.
use_cases.target_user: AI platform engineers adopting Tool Search

use_cases.title: Context Compaction Audit
use_cases.description: Emit rollback and transaction safety receipts when context compaction fails, proving original context is preserved and deferred registries are restored.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps teams running production agent pipelines

use_cases.title: Subagent Boundary Tracking
use_cases.description: Track what context crosses subagent boundaries, proving raw child output does not leak back to the parent context.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Tool Search Receipt
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the context-receipts skill to emit a receipt proving MCP tool index was loaded lazily, with startup token bucket and suppressed tool count.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick smoke test for lazy MCP loading

prompt_templates.title: Context Attention Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Using context-receipts, emit a context attention receipt showing which required context ids were delivered, acknowledged before planning, and cited before edits.
prompt_templates.scenario: Verify GraphRAG or memory context was actually used

prompt_templates.title: Compaction Transaction Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply context-receipts to a failed compaction scenario and emit rollback evidence showing original context preserved and system reminder queue restored.
prompt_templates.scenario: Audit failed context compaction for production safety

prompt_templates.title: Usage Attribution Breakdown
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-receipts to emit a usage attribution receipt mapping token buckets to categories like skills, subagents, plugins, and MCP servers with deferred and hydrated counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Debug high context consumption with privacy-safe attribution

output_examples.input: A user asks the agent to use MCP Tool Search to find a GitHub search tool and invoke it.
output: mcp.tool_index.loaded: 12 servers, 84 tools indexed, 0 full schemas loaded, startup under 1k tokens
mcp.tool_search.performed: query hashed, category repo_search, 5 candidates selected
mcp.tool_call.completed: args and results hashed, raw content not copied

output_examples.input: A compaction operation fails due to rate limiting while summarizing context.
output: context.compaction.summary.attempted: status failed, no candidate summary available
context.compaction.rollback.completed: swap not committed, original preserved, registries restored
context.compaction.transaction.completed: rolled back, post-tokens not recorded as success

best_practices: Always set raw_*_copied to false in receipts to prove private content stayed out of the audit trail
Use SHA-256 hashes for query, result, and component identifiers to enable deduplication without exposing content
Include an explicit audit_gap field in every receipt to document what the receipt does not prove semantically
anti_patterns: Including raw prompts, tool arguments, tool results, or customer names in receipt output
Emitting receipts that only say 'Tool Search enabled' without proving boundary behavior with counts and hashes
Recording post-compaction token counts as success when the summary call actually failed

faq.question: What is a context receipt?
faq.answer: A structured audit event that proves what crossed a context boundary in an AI agent workflow, using hashes, counts, and token buckets instead of raw private content.

faq.question: Does this skill execute any code?
faq.answer: No. It is a markdown documentation skill that defines event schemas and receipt patterns. The host agent or harness is responsible for emitting the actual events.

faq.question: How does it protect privacy?
faq.answer: Receipts use stable or hashed ids, token and line buckets, categorical reasons, and explicit raw_*_copied booleans. Raw prompts, schemas, args, results, and customer data are excluded by default.

faq.question: What is the audit_gap field?
faq.answer: An explicit field documenting what the receipt does not prove, such as whether the selected tool was semantically optimal for the task, or whether pruned text was semantically disposable.

faq.question: Can it verify compaction transaction safety?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill defines events for rollback evidence including whether the original context was preserved, deferred tool registries were restored, and post-tokens were not falsely recorded as success.

faq.question: Which AI tools is this compatible with?
faq.answer: It works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Any agent framework that can emit structured JSONL events can use these receipt patterns.
